description_long: This object consists of three open-ended wrenches, each made of metal and showing significant signs of use and age. The wrenches are arranged upright, leaning against each other to form a tripod-like structure, with their open jaws facing outward. The surfaces are heavily oxidized, displaying patches of rust, discoloration, and worn areas, especially along the handles and jaws. The wrenches have stamped size markings on their handles, though some are partially obscured by rust and wear. The metal is matte and rough, with no visible grip enhancements or rubber inserts. This set would typically be found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas, and is suitable for manual tightening or loosening of nuts and bolts.
description: A group of three old, rusted open-ended metal wrenches arranged upright in a tripod formation, with visible wear and stamped size markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open jaws of all three wrenches, with clear signs of rust and wear on the metal surfaces.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness of the wrenches and the way they lean together, with visible rust along the edges.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stamped size markings on the handles and the extent of surface corrosion.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, emphasizing the rough, aged texture and the overlapping arrangement of the wrenches.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Extensive rust and wear marks are visible across all surfaces, especially on the jaws and handles, with some areas showing deep oxidation and discoloration.
